rationale: Test fn test_rdkafka_fetch_long_poll added with both cases. Case A asserts 300ms<=poll_a<800ms on empty topic with fetch.min.bytes=1024/wait.max.ms=500; Case B asserts <300ms receive latency after a 2KB produce. Matches acceptance tolerances.

findings.summary: Case B uses 20ms poll granularity post-produce, which adds up to ~20ms jitter to poll_b — well within the 300ms budget but worth noting if the bound is later tightened.

Both cases create a fresh topic and drain a single init message to position the consumer at end-of-log; this is a reasonable way to ensure 'empty-ish' state without relying on broker auto-create timing.
